assets.notes: May 5 boss-specific rebuild/cutoff correction: the Oath-Eater is repacked as a four-frame 224px sheet from the V4 ChatGPT Images source board with wider left/right/bottom frame padding. Runtime frame cycling is slower and smoother, close-camera boss bias keeps the boss in frame during chase, and the boss charge movement interpolates along the telegraphed lane instead of snapping at impact. Default production-art path uses this asset; opt out with ?productionArt=0.

assets.intendedUse: Production Armistice Plaza large material-patch atlas for continuous civic stone, road, rubble, terminal, and breach terrain zones.
assets.licenseNote: PixelLab and ChatGPT Images terrain source art mechanically cropped, alpha cleaned, blended as larger patch frames, and packed with Python/Pillow; MIT-included for this project.
assets.notes: Armistice terrain cohesion pass: generated-source terrain cells are mechanically packed into six 256x128 material patches so runtime zones read as continuous authored surfaces instead of isolated stamped tiles.

assets.intendedUse: Production Armistice Plaza whole-map authored ground surface for coherent civic stone, road/asphalt, rubble, terminal, and breach material zones.
assets.licenseNote: ChatGPT Images terrain source art mechanically cropped, background-cleaned, resized, and composited into a whole-map runtime surface with Python/Pillow packaging only; MIT-included for this project.
assets.notes: May 5 terrain-only correction: the runtime authored ground rejects the single V4 sheet as the visible driver after close-camera proof showed it reading as a giant repeated cobble floor. The accepted pack now starts from a flat safety color instead of visible low-fidelity underpaint, fills the camera-relevant floor with dense overlapping V7 prop-pad-extension terrain pieces, and uses V6 interconnected terrain plates/strips as authored emphasis. The old V2/V4 broad sheets are no longer visible pixel sources, so the walkable floor better matches the prop-grounding pads in crack thickness, rubble scale, dirt grain, and contrast. A mechanical outer-silhouette alpha mask breaks up the PNG perimeter so the visible map edge reads jagged rather than square; props remain separate runtime sprites above this ground layer.

One generated cell with a vertical barricade-like object is intentionally excluded from runtime packing; the accepted cells provide civic plaza, asphalt, rubble, terminal floor, breach, and authored transition chunks.

assets.intendedUse: ChatGPT Images coherent whole-ground source for replacing the soft underpaint with a higher-fidelity continuous Armistice Plaza terrain layer.
assets.licenseNote: ChatGPT Images generated original AGI Armistice terrain source. Runtime use is limited to mechanical crop, resize, and compositing with accepted source plates; no procedural art creation.
assets.notes: Generated after live review found V3 terrain plates still read as pasted islands over a soft/dark underpaint. This source provides the continuous higher-fidelity base terrain with brighter midtone gameplay lanes for enemy readability.

assets.intendedUse: ChatGPT Images high-fidelity terrain plate atlas used as low-alpha calibration detail so base terrain matches prop-grounding fidelity without pasted rectangular mats.
assets.licenseNote: ChatGPT Images generated original AGI Armistice terrain plate source. Runtime use is limited to mechanical chroma-key cleanup, crop, resize, alpha edge cleanup, placement, and packing; no procedural art creation.
assets.notes: Generated after user feedback that the base terrain still did not match the prop-pad fidelity. The first attempted dominant placement was rejected for reading as separate islands; accepted runtime use demotes the plates to low-alpha material calibration over the coherent V2 base.

assets.intendedUse: ChatGPT Images high-fidelity interconnected terrain plate board used to raise the base terrain to match the prop-grounding pads without lowering pad fidelity.
assets.licenseNote: ChatGPT Images generated original AGI Armistice terrain plate source. Runtime use is limited to mechanical chroma-key cleanup, crop, resize, placement, and packing; no procedural art creation.
assets.notes: Accepted after the user clarified that prop pads should remain the fidelity bar. V6 supersedes the V5 runtime attempt because its isolated green-margin plates cut cleaner and reduce rectangular terrain boundaries.

assets.intendedUse: ChatGPT Images high-fidelity terrain transition strip board for connecting prop-pad-matched terrain plates into larger authored material runs.
assets.licenseNote: ChatGPT Images generated original AGI Armistice terrain strip source. Runtime use is limited to mechanical chroma-key cleanup, crop, resize, placement, and packing; no procedural art creation.
assets.notes: Used with V6 plates to fill the base terrain from several interconnected high-fidelity parts so terminal, drone, barricade, treaty, and breach pads read as local contact accents instead of imported mats.

assets.intendedUse: ChatGPT Images terrain-only prop-pad-extension board used to bring the base Armistice floor closer to the existing prop-grounding pad fidelity.

assets.notes: Generated after the user narrowed the remaining vertical-slice visual work to terrain only. V7 is not used as one huge floor sheet; accepted runtime packing uses it as overlapping ground-only extension pieces over the V6/V2 base so prop pads read less like separate mats.

assets.intendedUse: Runtime transparent atlas for Vector Lance, Signal Pulse, Context Saw, Patch Mortar, Causal Railgun, and passive/fusion draft icons.
assets.licenseNote: ChatGPT Images source art mechanically chroma-keyed, cropped, resized, and packed. No expressive weapon/protocol/fusion art was drawn in code.
assets.notes: Runtime path remains v1 for compatibility, but the atlas now derives from the stronger v2 animation source. Gives build-grammar additions source-backed visual identity and supports more animated Patch Mortar launch/arc/descent/impact timing, Signal Pulse echoes, Context Saw spin frames, Vector Lance, Causal Railgun, Coherence Indexer, Anchor Bodyguard, and Prediction Priority.

assets.intendedUse: Runtime 3-frame transparent extraction gate sheet for the Armistice post-clear exit objective.
assets.licenseNote: ChatGPT Images source art mechanically chroma-keyed, cropped, resized, and packed. No expressive gate art was drawn in code.
assets.notes: Appears only after Armistice's timer and boss-required clear are satisfied, giving the player a source-backed exit destination instead of an instant summary transition.
